description: Winter season crops requiring cool climate for growth

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Wheat
steps: Select well-drained loamy or clay loam soil with pH 6.5–7.5.
Deep plough with disc plough to 25–30 cm depth to break hardpan.
Apply 10 tonnes/hectare of well-decomposed FYM (farmyard manure) and incorporate.
Cross harrow 2–3 times to achieve fine tilth; use cultivator followed by planking.
Ensure soil is weed-free and has good moisture retention.
Pre-sowing irrigation (rauni) 7–10 days before sowing for proper germination.

step_2_seed_selection_and_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Select certified high-yielding variety suitable for your region (HD-2967, PBW-343, GW-322).
Seed rate: 100–125 kg/ha for normal sowing; 150 kg/ha for late sowing.
Treat seeds with Carbendazim 50 WP @ 2g/kg seed OR Thiram 75 WS @ 2.5g/kg seed to prevent loose smut.
For termite control, treat with Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 4 ml/kg seed.
Bio-inoculate with Azotobacter 600 g/ha + PSB 600 g/ha mixed in 10% jaggery solution.
Dry treated seeds in shade for 30 minutes before sowing.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing Method
steps: Use seed-cum-fertilizer drill for precise sowing at 4–5 cm depth.
Row spacing: 20–22.5 cm for normal sowing; 18 cm for late sowing.
Ensure continuous seed drop without gaps; target plant population of 125–150 plants/sq.m.
Apply basal fertilizer: DAP 125 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha + Urea 65 kg/ha in furrows.
After sowing, plank the field to press soil for better seed-soil contact.
If soil moisture is inadequate, give light irrigation immediately after sowing.

step_4_irrigation_schedule.title: Irrigation Management
steps: Irrigation 1 (Crown Root Initiation - CRI): Day 20–25 after sowing; critical stage for tillering.
Irrigation 2 (Tillering): Day 40–45; apply 75 mm water.
Irrigation 3 (Late Tillering): Day 60–65; maintain soil moisture.
Irrigation 4 (Flowering): Day 85–90; most critical — do not allow moisture stress.
Irrigation 5 (Grain Filling): Day 110–115; apply light irrigation 50 mm.
Irrigation 6 (Dough stage): Day 130–135 (optional; depends on soil moisture).
Stop irrigation 15 days before harvest for uniform ripening.
Each irrigation: 60–75 mm water; total 450 mm.

schedule.fertilizers: DAP 125 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha + Urea 65 kg/ha (N:P:K = 30:60:20 kg/ha)
schedule.method: Apply in furrows below seed level
Broadcast in standing crop before irrigation

schedule.fertilizers: Urea 43 kg/ha (20 kg N/ha) - optional for high-yield varieties
Zinc Sulphate 25 kg/ha basal if Zn deficiency; Boron 0.2% foliar at flowering

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Control
steps: Identify major weeds: Phalaris minor (canary grass), Chenopodium, Anagallis.
Pre-emergence: Apply Pendimethalin 30 EC @ 3 litres/ha within 3 days of sowing.
Post-emergence (Day 30-35): Apply Clodinafop-propargyl 15 WP @ 400 g/ha + Metsulfuron 20 WP @ 20 g/ha for mixed weeds.
For Phalaris minor resistant strains: Use Sulfosulfuron 75 WG @ 250 g/ha at Day 30-35.
Manual weeding at Day 30 and Day 50 for residual weeds.
Ensure herbicide application at proper soil moisture for best absorption.

pests.symptoms: Honeydew secretion, sooty mold, stunted growth
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha OR Dimethoate 30 EC @ 500 ml/ha; spray when threshold reached

pests.symptoms: Drying of plants; can pull plants easily
pests.control: Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 4 litres/ha as soil drench; or Phorate 10G @ 10 kg/ha at sowing
pests.timing: At sowing or when symptoms appear

pests.control: Spray Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha OR Quinalphos 25 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha
Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha OR Spinosad 45 SC @ 73 ml/ha

diseases.symptoms: Brown/yellow pustules on leaves and stems
diseases.control: Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha OR Tebuconazole 250 EC @ 500 ml/ha; spray at appearance of first pustules
Seed treatment with Carbendazim 50 WP @ 2g/kg seed OR Vitavax 200 @ 2g/kg

diseases.symptoms: Partial blackening of grain; fishy odor
diseases.control: Spray Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha at ear emergence; use resistant varieties
Wettable Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Tebuconazole 250 EC @ 400 ml/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Chlorothalonil 75 WP @ 1.5 kg/ha
diseases.timing: At disease appearance; repeat after 10 days

step_9_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when grain moisture is 20-25% and straw turns golden yellow.
Check maturity: Grains hard, not easily pressed; straw brittle.
Use combine harvester for large fields; manual harvesting with sickle for small farms.
Cut at 10-15 cm above ground level.
For manual harvest: Bind into small sheaves; stack for 3-5 days for drying.
Thresh using power thresher @ 500-600 RPM to avoid grain cracking.
Winnow to separate grain from chaff using winnowing fan or combine's cleaning system.
Expected yield: 4.5–6 tonnes/ha (irrigated); 2.5–3.5 tonnes/ha (rainfed).

step_10_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Sun-dry grain on clean tarpaulin to reduce moisture to 12% (takes 3-5 days).
Test moisture: Grain should crack when bitten, not flatten.
Clean grain using seed cleaner/grader to remove weed seeds and broken grains.
Grade by size and test weight (minimum 78 kg/hl).
Store in jute bags lined with polythene or in metallic silos.
Treat storage area with Malathion 50 EC @ 5 ml/litre on walls and floor.
Fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide tablets (1 tablet/tonne) in sealed storage for 5 days.
Stack bags on wooden pallets, 20 cm away from walls for ventilation.
Monitor temperature and moisture monthly; moisture >12% requires re-drying.
Straw management: Chop straw; use as cattle feed or incorporate into soil.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Barley
steps: Select well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil; pH 6.0–8.0; tolerates salinity better than wheat.
Deep plough to 20-25 cm using disc plough.
Apply 5-8 tonnes/ha FYM and incorporate with cultivator.
Cross harrow 2-3 times for fine tilth; planking for level field.
Ensure field is weed-free and has adequate moisture for germination.

step_2_seed_selection_and_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Select certified varieties: BH-902, BH-393, RD-2035 (feed barley) or DWRUB-52 (malt barley).
Seed rate: 100 kg/ha for normal; 120 kg/ha for late sowing.
Treat with Carbendazim 50 WP @ 2g/kg seed OR Thiram 75 WS @ 2.5g/kg seed.
For loose smut control, Vitavax 200 @ 2g/kg seed.
Bio-inoculate with Azospirillum 600 g/ha + PSB 600 g/ha in jaggery solution.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing Method
steps: Use seed drill or broadcast method for small fields.
Row spacing: 22-25 cm for grain; 18 cm for fodder barley.
Sowing depth: 3-5 cm in heavy soil; 4-6 cm in light soil.
Seed rate: 100 kg/ha; target 250-300 plants/sq.m.
Apply basal: DAP 100 kg/ha + Urea 43 kg/ha + MOP 20 kg/ha in furrows.
Plank after sowing for moisture conservation.

step_4_irrigation_schedule.title: Irrigation Management
steps: Irrigation 1 (CRI stage): Day 20-25 after sowing — critical for tillering.
Irrigation 2 (Late tillering): Day 45-50; apply 60 mm water.
Irrigation 3 (Flowering): Day 75-80; most critical for grain formation.
Irrigation 4 (Grain filling): Day 105-110 (optional; depends on rainfall).
Stop irrigation 10-12 days before harvest.
Barley requires 30-40% less water than wheat; avoid over-irrigation.

schedule.fertilizers: N:P:K = 40:40:20 kg/ha (DAP 87 kg + Urea 43 kg + MOP 33 kg)
Urea 43 kg/ha (20 kg N) - for malt barley reduce to 10 kg N

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Control
steps: Pre-emergence: Pendimethalin 30 EC @ 2.5 litres/ha within 3 days of sowing.
Post-emergence (Day 30-35): Apply Sulfosulfuron 75 WG @ 250 g/ha for Phalaris minor.
For broadleaf weeds: 2,4-D 80 WP @ 1 kg/ha at Day 30-35.
Manual weeding: One hand weeding at Day 35-40 if herbicides not used.

pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha; occurs during February-March

step_8_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when grain moisture is 18-20%; straw turns yellow, ears droop.
Use combine harvester for large fields.
For malt barley: Harvest at 14-15% moisture to preserve germination.
Thresh at 500 RPM to avoid grain cracking.
Expected yield: 3.5–5 tonnes/ha (feed); 3–4 tonnes/ha (malt).

step_9_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Dry grain to 12% moisture for feed; 11% for malt barley.
Clean and grade; remove barley awns (beards).
Store in gunny bags; fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide.
Malt barley requires separate storage at 10-15°C, 60% RH.
Straw used as cattle feed; malt barley processed for brewing.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Oats
steps: Select well-drained loamy to clay loam soil; pH 5.5–7.0.
Deep plough to 20-25 cm once; cross harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 8-10 tonnes/ha FYM and incorporate.
Form fine tilth; level with planking.

step_2_seed_selection.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Use certified varieties: OS-6, OS-7, Kent, JHO-822 (grain) or OL-9 (fodder).
Seed rate: 100-120 kg/ha for grain; 120-150 kg/ha for fodder.
Treat with Thiram 75 WS @ 2.5g/kg seed + Carbendazim 1g/kg.
No Rhizobium needed (cereal).

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing Method
steps: Row spacing: 25-30 cm for grain; 20-25 cm for fodder.
Sowing depth: 3-5 cm using seed drill.
Basal: N:P:K = 40:40:20 kg/ha.
Target plant population: 200-250 plants/sq.m.

step_4_irrigation_and_fertilizer.title: Irrigation & Fertilizer
irrigation_steps: Total 3-4 irrigations; stop 10 days before harvest.

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when grain is hard and straw turns light yellow.
For fodder: Harvest at 50% flowering stage (Day 80-90).
Use combine harvester for grain; sickle for fodder.
Expected grain yield: 2.5-4 tonnes/ha; fodder yield: 25-35 tonnes/ha green.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry grain to 12% moisture.
Store in gunny bags; oats store well due to hulls.
Fodder: Make silage or dry as hay for cattle feed.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Chickpea (Gram/Chana)
steps: Select well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil; pH 6.0–7.5.
Avoid waterlogged or saline soils; chickpea is sensitive to waterlogging.
Deep plough once to 20-25 cm; cross harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 5-6 tonnes/ha FYM; incorporate with final harrowing.
Form broad beds and furrows (BBF) for drainage in heavy soils.
Ensure fine tilth and weed-free seedbed.

step_2_seed_selection_and_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Select certified varieties: Desi type (BG-256, JG-11) or Kabuli (L-550, JGK-1).
Seed rate: 80-100 kg/ha for Desi; 100-120 kg/ha for Kabuli.
Treat with Carbendazim 50 WP @ 2g/kg + Thiram 75 WS @ 2g/kg for wilt control.
Inoculate with Mesorhizobium (strain IC-76) @ 200 g/10 kg seed in 10% jaggery solution.
Inoculate with PSB 200 g/10 kg seed simultaneously.
Dry inoculated seed in shade for 30 minutes before sowing.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing Method
steps: Use seed drill or desi plough for line sowing.
Row spacing: 30-45 cm (Desi); 45-60 cm (Kabuli).
Plant spacing: 10-12 cm (thinning to 8-10 cm at Day 15-20).
Sowing depth: 5-6 cm in light soil; 3-4 cm in heavy soil.
Apply basal: P2O5 40-50 kg/ha + K2O 20 kg/ha; starter N 15 kg/ha.
Do not apply high N (reduces nodulation).
Target population: 33-40 plants/sq.m.

step_4_irrigation_management.title: Irrigation Schedule
steps: Pre-sowing irrigation if soil moisture is low.
Critical irrigation 1: At flowering (Day 50-60) — essential for pod set.
Critical irrigation 2: At pod filling (Day 80-90) if prolonged dry spell.
Avoid irrigation at vegetative stage — promotes excessive growth and disease.
Avoid waterlogging at all stages — causes root rot.
Stop irrigation 15-20 days before harvest.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 250-312 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha (P2O5 40-50 kg + K2O 20 kg)
schedule.method: Band placement 5 cm below and 5 cm beside seed

schedule.fertilizers: Urea 33 kg/ha (15 kg N) - do not exceed
Gypsum 200 kg/ha if calcium deficiency (poor pod filling)

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Control
steps: Pre-emergence: Pendimethalin 30 EC @ 3 litres/ha within 3 days of sowing.
Pre-emergence alternative: Metolachlor 50 EC @ 2 litres/ha for broad spectrum.
Post-emergence (Day 25-30): Quizalofop-ethyl 5 EC @ 1 litre/ha for grassy weeds.
For broadleaf weeds: Imazethapyr 10 SL @ 1 litre/ha at Day 25-30.
Manual weeding: One hand weeding at Day 25-30, second at Day 45-50.
Avoid deep cultivation near roots (nodule damage).

pests.symptoms: Larvae bore into pods; feeds on developing grains
pests.control: Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha OR Indoxacarb 14.5 SC @ 500 ml/ha at pod initiation
pests.alternative: HaNPV 250 LE/ha + 0.1% Teepol; apply at evening hours

pests.control: Quinalphos 25 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha OR Spinosad 45 SC @ 73 ml/ha

pests.symptoms: Stunted growth; sooty mold on leaves
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha OR Dimethoate 30 EC @ 500 ml/ha

pests.symptoms: Plants cut at base near ground level
pests.control: Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha soil drench at base

diseases.control: Use resistant varieties (JG-11, BG-256); Carbendazim seed treatment; Trichoderma soil application @ 2.5 kg/ha in FYM

diseases.symptoms: Black lesions on taproot; plant wilting
diseases.control: Seed treatment with Trichoderma viride 4g/kg; soil drenching with Carbendazim 0.1%

diseases.symptoms: Circular brown spots with concentric rings on leaves; girdling of stem
diseases.control: Chlorothalonil 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2.5 kg/ha; spray at 10-day intervals

diseases.symptoms: Grayish fungal growth on branches and pods
diseases.control: Carbendazim 50 WP @ 500 g/ha + Mancozeb 75 WP @ 1.5 kg/ha; avoid dense sowing
Rogue out infected plants; control leafhopper vectors

step_9_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when 80% pods turn brown and leaves start shedding.
Grains should be hard and rattling inside pods.
Cut plants 10-15 cm above ground using sickle.
Windrow plants in small heaps for 5-7 days for complete drying.
Thresh using thresher @ 400-500 RPM (slow speed to avoid grain cracking).
For Kabuli chickpea, extra care to avoid seed coat peeling.
Winnow to separate grain from chaff using winnowing fan.
Expected yield: 1.5–2.5 tonnes/ha (irrigated); 1–1.5 tonnes/ha (rainfed).

step_10_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Sun-dry grain to 10-12% moisture (grains should break with snap, not bend).
Grading: Kabuli > 8mm diameter; Desi > 6mm.
Store in clean, dry, cool godowns (20-25°C).
Treat storage area with Malathion 50 EC @ 5 ml/litre.
For long-term storage, fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide (1 tablet/tonne) for 5-7 days.
Store in gunny bags stacked on wooden pallets; avoid direct floor contact.
For seed purpose, treat with Carbendazim + Thiram before storage.
Pulse beetle (Callosobruchus): Add Neem leaf powder @ 1% to stored grain.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Lentil (Masoor)
steps: Select well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil; pH 6.0–7.5.
Lentil sensitive to waterlogging; avoid heavy clay soils.
Deep plough once to 20-25 cm; cross harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 4-5 tonnes/ha FYM and incorporate.
Form fine tilth; level field with planking.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Use certified varieties: PL-5, PL-406, IPL-81, HUL-57.
Seed rate: 40-50 kg/ha (bold); 30-40 kg/ha (small seeded).
Treat with Carbendazim 50 WP @ 2g/kg + Thiram 75 WS @ 2g/kg for wilt and root rot.
Inoculate with Rhizobium leguminosarum @ 200 g/10 kg seed.
Inoculate with PSB 200 g/10 kg seed.
Shade-dry inoculated seed for 30 minutes.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing Method
steps: Row spacing: 25-30 cm (normal); 20-25 cm (late sowing).
Plant spacing: 8-10 cm after thinning.
Sowing depth: 3-4 cm (lentil has small seeds).
Seed rate: 40 kg/ha; target 150-200 plants/sq.m.
Basal: P2O5 40-50 kg/ha + K2O 20 kg/ha; starter N 15 kg/ha.
Do not apply basal N >20 kg/ha (inhibits nodulation).

step_4_irrigation_and_fertilizer.title: Irrigation & Fertilizer Schedule
irrigation_steps: Irrigation 2: At pod filling (Day 70-80) if severe dry spell.
Avoid irrigation at vegetative stage and near maturity.

fertilizer_schedule.fertilizers: SSP 250 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha + Urea 22 kg/ha

pests.control: Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha at pod initiation

diseases.control: Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha at first appearance
Carbendazim seed treatment; use resistant variety

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when 80% pods turn brown and plants dry.
Cut plants at base; dry in sun for 5-7 days.
Thresh carefully (lentil seeds crack easily).
Expected yield: 1–1.8 tonnes/ha.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry to 10-12% moisture.
Clean to remove broken seeds and stones.
Store in gunny bags; fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide.
Lentil has good storage life (12-18 months).

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Field Pea (Matar)
steps: Select well-drained sandy loam to clay loam; pH 6.0–7.5.
Deep plough once; cross harrow 2 times.
Apply 6-8 tonnes/ha FYM.
Form fine, level seedbed.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Use varieties: Pusa Pragati, Pusa Mukta, Kashi Nandini (vegetable pea).
Seed rate: 80-100 kg/ha (for grain); 120-150 kg/ha (for vegetable).
Treat with Carbendazim 50 WP @ 2g/kg + Thiram 75 WS @ 2g/kg.
Inoculate with Rhizobium leguminosarum @ 200 g/10 kg seed + PSB 200 g/10 kg seed.
For vegetable pea, treat with Tebuconazole 2 ml/kg for powdery mildew.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing Method
steps: Row spacing: 30-45 cm (grain); 45-60 cm (vegetable with trellis).
Plant spacing: 10-12 cm after thinning.
Sowing depth: 4-5 cm.
Basal: N:P:K = 20:50:30 kg/ha (P2O5 50 kg, K2O 30 kg).
Target 50-60 plants/sq.m. for grain; 30-40 for vegetable.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation Schedule
steps: Irrigation 1: Pre-sowing for germination.
Irrigation 2: At pre-flowering (Day 40-45) — critical.
Irrigation 3: At pod formation (Day 65-70).
Irrigation 4: At pod filling (Day 85-90) if dry.
Avoid waterlogging; pea is sensitive to excess water.
For vegetable pea, maintain even moisture for tender pods.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 312 kg/ha + MOP 50 kg/ha + Urea 43 kg/ha
Urea 65 kg/ha at pod initiation for better yield

diseases.control: Tebuconazole 250 EC @ 500 ml/ha OR Wettable Sulphur @ 2 kg/ha
Seed treatment with Carbendazim; use wilt-resistant varieties
Control aphid vector; rogue infected plants

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_7_harvesting.timing: Grain pea: Day 106–110; Vegetable pea: Day 60–80
steps: Grain pea: Harvest when 85% pods dry and brown.
Vegetable pea: Harvest tender, bright green pods before seeds mature.
Pick pods every 5-7 days for vegetable pea.
Thresh grain pea at 400-500 RPM.
Expected yield: 2-3 tonnes/ha (grain); 8-12 tonnes/ha (green pods).

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry grain to 10-12% moisture; store in gunny bags.
Vegetable pea: Pre-cool to 4°C within 2 hours; store at 0-2°C, 95% RH for 2-3 weeks.
Frozen or canned for processing; garden peas dried for later use.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Lathyrus (Khesari)
steps: Tolerates poor, heavy clay, and even waterlogged soils.
Minimum tillage sufficient (rice fallow crop).
Direct drilling in rice stubble is common.
No ploughing needed if following rice; just remove stubble.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Use varieties: Nirmal, Prateek, Mahateora (low ODAP content).
Seed rate: 60-80 kg/ha.
Treat with Carbendazim 2g/kg seed for wilt control.
Rhizobium inoculation recommended but not mandatory (crop has native rhizobia).

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing Method
steps: Sow directly into standing rice stubble (relay cropping).
Row spacing: 25-30 cm; depth 3-4 cm.
Basal: P2O5 30 kg/ha + K2O 15 kg/ha; no N required.
No irrigation (grown on residual moisture).

step_4_irrigation_and_fertilizer.title: Irrigation & Fertilizer
steps: No irrigation required — grown entirely on residual soil moisture.
If severe drought, one irrigation at flowering beneficial.
Fertilizer: Only basal P and K; no N needed.

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when 90% pods turn brown.
Cut plants at base; sun-dry for 5-7 days.
Thresh by beating; winnow.
Expected yield: 1–1.8 tonnes/ha.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry to 10% moisture.
Store in gunny bags; good storage life.
WARNING: Prolonged consumption causes lathyrism (neurological disorder) — consume as part of mixed diet, not as staple.
Low-ODAP varieties (Nirmal) are safe for consumption.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Mustard (Sarson)
steps: Select well-drained loamy to clay loam soil; pH 6.0–7.5.
Deep plough once to 25-30 cm; cross harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 5-8 tonnes/ha FYM and incorporate.
Form fine tilth; level with planking.
For irrigated condition, make ridges and furrows at 45 cm spacing.

step_2_seed_selection_and_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Select certified varieties: Pusa Bold, Pusa Mustard-21, RH-30, RH-749.
Seed rate: 4-5 kg/ha (irrigated); 5-6 kg/ha (rainfed).
Treat with Carbendazim 50 WP @ 2g/kg + Thiram 75 WS @ 2g/kg seed.
For aphid control, treat with Imidacloprid 70 WS @ 5 ml/kg seed.
Bio-inoculate with Azotobacter 600 g/ha + PSB 600 g/ha.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing Method
steps: Use seed drill or broadcasting (broadcast followed by planking).
Row spacing: 45 cm (irrigated); 30 cm (rainfed).
Plant spacing: 10-12 cm after thinning.
Sowing depth: 2-4 cm (mustard has small seeds).
Basal: N:P:K = 40:40:20 kg/ha.
Target 50-60 plants/sq.m.

step_4_irrigation_schedule.title: Irrigation Management
steps: Irrigation 1: Pre-sowing (rauni) for germination.
Irrigation 2: At rosette stage (Day 25-30).
Irrigation 3: At flowering (Day 60-65) — most critical.
Irrigation 4: At pod filling (Day 100-105) — improves oil content.
Avoid irrigation during maturity (10-15 days before harvest).

schedule.fertilizers: DAP 87 kg + MOP 33 kg + Urea 43 kg per ha (N:P:K = 40:40:20)
Urea 43 kg/ha (20 kg N) - optional for high-yield
Gypsum 200 kg/ha OR Elemental Sulphur 40 kg/ha

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Control
steps: Identify weeds: Chenopodium, Anagallis, Vicia, Phalaris minor.
Pre-emergence: Pendimethalin 30 EC @ 3 litres/ha within 3 days of sowing.
Alternative pre-emergence: Oxyfluorfen 23.5 EC @ 0.5 litres/ha.
Post-emergence (Day 25-30): Quizalofop-ethyl 5 EC @ 1 litre/ha for grassy weeds.
For broadleaf weeds: Clopyralid 30 EC @ 200 ml/ha at Day 25-30.
Manual weeding: One inter-cultivation at Day 30, another at Day 50.

pests.symptoms: Honeydew secretion; sooty mold; stunted plants
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha OR Dimethoate 30 EC @ 500 ml/ha
pests.timing: December–January (flowering stage); repeat after 10-15 days if needed

pests.symptoms: Leaf skeletonization; larvae feed on leaves
pests.control: Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha OR Quinalphos 25 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha
Spray Cypermethrin 10 EC @ 500 ml/ha OR Deltamethrin 2.8 EC @ 300 ml/ha

pests.symptoms: Bore holes in pods; missing seeds
pests.control: Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha at pod formation

diseases.symptoms: White pustules on leaves and stems; staghead on inflorescence
diseases.control: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre OR Ridomil MZ @ 2.5 kg/ha

diseases.symptoms: Yellow patches on upper leaf; purple-white down on lower surface
diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha OR Fosetyl-Al 80 WP @ 2.5 kg/ha

diseases.symptoms: Dark brown concentric spots on leaves and pods
diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha + Carbendazim 50 WP @ 500 g/ha

diseases.symptoms: Watery soft rot of stem; white mycelium
diseases.control: Carbendazim 50 WP @ 1 kg/ha drench; avoid dense sowing

step_9_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when 75-80% pods turn yellow-brown and seeds become hard.
Cut plants at base using sickle.
Windrow for 5-7 days for complete drying.
Thresh using thresher at 400-500 RPM.
Winnow to separate seed from chaff.
Expected yield: 1.5–2.5 tonnes/ha (irrigated); 1–1.5 tonnes/ha (rainfed).
Oil content: 38-42% of seed weight.

step_10_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Sun-dry seed to 6-8% moisture for safe storage.
Clean using seed cleaner to remove broken seeds and debris.
Store in cool, dry, well-ventilated warehouse.
Treat storage area with Malathion 50 EC @ 5 ml/litre on walls.
Fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide (1 tablet/tonne) for 5-7 days.
Mustard oil extraction: Cold press (kachi ghani) or solvent extraction.
Oil cake (meal) used as cattle feed (contains 30-35% protein).
Market price depends on oil content (higher oil → premium).

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Rapeseed
steps: Same as mustard; prefers light to medium loamy soil.
Deep plough once; harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 5-6 tonnes/ha FYM.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
steps: Varieties: T-9, Bhavani, Pusa Kranti.
Seed rate: 5-6 kg/ha.
Treat with Carbendazim 2g/kg + Thiram 2g/kg.
Row spacing: 30-40 cm; depth 2-3 cm.
Sowing window: October 15–November 15.

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when pods turn yellow-brown.
Thresh and winnow.
Expected yield: 1.2–2 tonnes/ha.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Linseed (Flaxseed)
steps: Deep, well-drained loamy soil; pH 5.5–7.0.
Deep plough to 25-30 cm; harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 5 tonnes/ha FYM.
Fine, weed-free seedbed required.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
steps: Varieties: Neelum, Kiran, Garima (oil type); Jawahar-7 (fiber type).
Seed rate: 40-50 kg/ha (oil); 80-100 kg/ha (fiber).
Treat with Carbendazim 2g/kg + Thiram 2g/kg.
Row spacing: 25-30 cm (oil); 15-20 cm (fiber).
Depth: 2-3 cm; sowing window: October–November.

step_3_irrigation_and_fertilizer.title: Irrigation & Fertilizer
irrigation_steps: Critical at flowering (Day 60-70) and capsule formation (Day 90-100).

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Oil flax: Harvest when 80% capsules turn brown.
Fiber flax: Harvest at 50% capsule maturity (straw quality declines after).
Pull entire plant (not cut) for fiber type to retain fiber length.
Thresh for oil seed; retting for fiber extraction.
Expected yield: 1–1.5 tonnes/ha seed; 5–7 tonnes/ha straw (fiber).

step_6_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry seed to 8% moisture.
Store in cool, dark place (high oil content — prone to rancidity).
Oil extraction: Cold pressed (flaxseed oil) high in omega-3.
Fiber: Water retting 10-14 days → scutching → combing.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Safflower (Kusum)
steps: Deep, well-drained black cotton soil or red loam; pH 7.0–8.0.
Deep plough to 30-35 cm (deep-rooted).
Apply 5-6 tonnes/ha FYM.
Form ridges and furrows for drainage.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
steps: Varieties: A-1, Bhima, NARI-6, PBNS-12.
Seed rate: 12-15 kg/ha.
Treat with Carbendazim 2g/kg + Thiram 2g/kg.
Row spacing: 45-60 cm; plant spacing: 15-20 cm.
Depth: 5-6 cm; sowing: October–November.

step_3_irrigation_and_fertilizer.title: Irrigation & Fertilizer
irrigation_steps: Critical at rosette stage, flowering (Day 60-80), and capitulum formation.
3-4 irrigations; drought tolerant but responds to irrigation.

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when 90% leaves dry and seeds rattle in capitulum.
Cut capitula with 10-15 cm stalk.
Sun-dry; thresh by beating.
Expected yield: 1.2–2 tonnes/ha seed.
Safflower oil: 28-32% oil content; high in linoleic acid.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Sunflower (Rabi Type)
steps: Well-drained sandy loam to clay loam; pH 6.0–8.0.
Deep plough once; harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 8-10 tonnes/ha FYM.
Form fine seedbed.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Selection & Sowing
steps: Use hybrid varieties: KBSH-44, KBSH-53, DRSH-1, LSFH-171.
Seed rate: 6-8 kg/ha (hybrid); 10-12 kg/ha (OPV).
Treat with Carbendazim 2g/kg + Thiram 2g/kg.
Row spacing: 60 cm; plant spacing: 30 cm.
Sowing depth: 3-5 cm; sowing: October–November.

step_3_irrigation_and_fertilizer.title: Irrigation & Fertilizer
irrigation_steps: Critical: At button stage (Day 35-40), flowering (Day 55-60), and seed filling.
Drip irrigation highly beneficial (30% water saving).

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when back of head turns yellow-brown and seeds have black stripes.
Cut heads with 20-30 cm stalk.
Sun-dry heads for 3-5 days; thresh by beating or using maize sheller.
Expected yield: 1.5–2.5 tonnes/ha.

step_6_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry seed to 8-10% moisture.
Store in gunny bags; fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide.
Oil content: 40-45%; high-quality edible oil.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Sugarcane (Rabi/Spring Planting)
steps: Deep, well-drained loamy or black cotton soil; pH 6.5–7.5.
Deep plough to 35-40 cm using disc plough; subsoiling every 3 years.
Apply 25 tonnes/ha FYM + 2 tonnes/ha compost.
Form furrows at 75-90 cm spacing (depending on variety).
Incorporate lime if pH < 6.0 @ 2 tonnes/ha.

step_2_sett_preparation.title: Seed Sett (Cane) Preparation
steps: Select disease-free, healthy 8-10 month old canes from certified nursery.
Cut 2-3 budded setts (25-30 cm long) using sharp, disinfected blade.
Treat setts with Aretan 6 0.25% OR Bavistin 0.1% solution for 5 minutes.
Hot Water Treatment: 50°C for 2 hours for ratoon stunting disease.
Sett rate: 35,000-40,000 setts/ha (3-budded, 30 cm gap).

step_3_planting.title: Planting Method
steps: Place setts end-to-end in furrows at 5-8 cm depth.
Spacing: 75-90 cm between rows; setts touching end-to-end in row.
Cover with 5 cm soil; firm with foot.
Basal: N:P:K = 80:80:80 kg/ha.
Apply 500 kg/ha gypsum in furrows for calcium.
Light irrigation immediately after planting to settle soil.

step_4_irrigation_management.title: Irrigation Schedule
steps: Germination (Day 0-30): Irrigate every 5-7 days (70-80% SMC).
Tillering (Day 31-120): Every 7-10 days; 75 mm each.
Grand Growth (Day 121-270): Every 10-15 days; 80 mm each.
Maturity (Day 271-365): Reduce to every 15-20 days.
Total: 20-25 irrigations; each 60-80 mm.
Drip irrigation at 80% ETc saves 30-40% water.
Stop irrigation 3-4 weeks before harvest.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 500 kg/ha + MOP 133 kg/ha + Urea 87 kg/ha (N:P:K = 40:80:80)
Urea 174 kg/ha (80 kg N) + MOP 67 kg/ha (40 kg K)
ZnSO4 25 kg/ha + FeSO4 20 kg/ha + Borax 10 kg/ha at planting
Silica 100 kg/ha at planting (improves stalk strength)

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Control
steps: Pre-emergence (Day 0-3): Atrazine 50 WP @ 2 kg/ha + Alachlor 50 EC @ 2 litres/ha.
Post-emergence (Day 30): 2,4-D 80 WP @ 1.25 kg/ha for broadleaf weeds.
Manual weeding at Day 30, 60, and 90.
Earthing up at Day 90-120 (suppresses weeds, supports stalks).
Trash mulching after 1st year suppresses subsequent weeds.

pests.control: Carbofuran 3G @ 33 kg/ha at Day 30-45; drench Chlorpyriphos 20 EC 2 litres/ha
Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha foliar at Day 90-120
Phorate 10G @ 25 kg/ha at planting; Chlorpyriphos soil drench
Endosulfan 35 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha OR Imidacloprid 100 ml/ha
Quinalphos 25 EC @ 2 litres/ha; release Epiricania melanoleuca (biocontrol)

diseases.control: Hot water sett treatment 50°C/2hrs; Carbendazim 0.1% sett dip; use resistant variety
Hot water treatment; rogue out infected plants (whip stage)
Use HWT (50°C, 2 hrs); use disease-free planting material
Use resistant variety; Carbendazim 0.1% sett treatment
Rogue infected plants; control leafhopper vectors

step_9_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Check maturity: Brix > 18% and pol% > 14% using refractometer.
De-trash (remove dry leaves) 2-3 months before harvest.
Stop irrigation 3-4 weeks before harvest.
Cut canes at ground level; remove tops and leaves.
Transport to mill within 24 hours (sucrose reduces with time).
Expected yield: 80-120 tonnes/ha cane.
Sugar recovery: 10-12% of cane weight.

step_10_ratoon_management.title: Ratoon Crop Management (for next 2-3 years)
steps: After harvest, cut stubs flush with ground using sharp blade.
Apply 25 tonnes/ha FYM around stubs.
Gap filling: plant fresh setts in gaps where germination poor.
Apply full fertilizer dose same as plant crop.
Irrigate immediately after harvest for sprouting.
Ratoon yield: 60-80% of plant crop; maximum 3 ratoons recommended.

step_11_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest & Mill Processing
steps: Trash (dry leaves) used for mulching next crop.
Press mud (mill byproduct) returned to field as manure (5-10 tonnes/ha).
Bagasse used for co-generation in mill (electricity).
Molasses used for alcohol/ethanol production.
Sugar processing: Crushing → Clarification → Evaporation → Crystallization → Centrifugation.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Tobacco (Rabi Type)
steps: Light sandy loam to medium loam soil; pH 5.5–7.0.
Deep plough to 30 cm; 3-4 harrowings for fine tilth.
Apply 10 tonnes/ha FYM; incorporate.
Form raised beds 1.2 m wide for drainage.

step_2_nursery_preparation.title: Nursery (Seedling Raising)
steps: Prepare raised nursery beds: 1 m × 10 m size.
Sterilize soil with Formalin 2% solution 15 days before sowing; cover with polythene for 7 days.
Sow seeds very thinly: 6-8 g/100 sq.m; cover with fine soil.
Spray Captan 0.2% after sowing.
Transplant at 45-55 days (height 15-20 cm, 4-5 leaves).

step_3_transplanting.title: Transplanting
steps: Transplant in cool hours (morning or evening).
Spacing: 90 cm × 60 cm (flue-cured); 75 cm × 45 cm (burley).
Apply basal fertilizer in furrows: N:P:K = 80:60:80 kg/ha.
Drench transplanted seedlings with Carbendazim 0.1% for root protection.
Light irrigation immediately after transplanting.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation Management
steps: At transplanting: Light irrigation immediately.
Vegetative (Day 61-100): Every 7-10 days; 40-50 mm each.
Maturity (Day 101-125): Reduce water.
Stop 2 weeks before harvest.
Total 6-8 irrigations.

step_5_fertilizer.title: Fertilizer Schedule

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 375 kg/ha + MOP 133 kg/ha + Urea 87 kg/ha

step_5_fertilizer.note: Do NOT over-apply N — reduces leaf quality and burning characteristics.

step_6_cultural_practices.title: Special Cultural Practices
steps: Topping (Day 70-75): Remove growing apex when 50% plants start flowering.
Desuckering (weekly after topping): Remove suckers from leaf axils.
Apply MH-30 (Maleic Hydrazide 0.35%) to retard sucker growth.
Remove lowest 2-3 ground leaves (sand leaves) — they reduce quality.

pests.control: SLNPV 250 LE/ha OR Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha

diseases.control: Wash hands with soap before handling; remove infected plants

step_8_harvesting_and_curing.title: Harvesting & Curing
step_8_harvesting_and_curing.timing: Day 126–130; leaves harvested bottom to top over 8-10 weeks
steps: Harvest leaves at 75-80% maturity (light green to yellow-green).
Pick 2-3 leaves per plant per week from bottom upwards.
Flue-cured tobacco: String leaves on sticks; cure in barn at 35°C → 70°C over 5-7 days.
Air-cured (burley): Hang in open barn for 30-45 days.
Sun-cured: Spread on tarpaulin; 2-3 weeks sun drying.
Expected yield: 1.5-2.5 tonnes/ha cured leaf.

step_9_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Sort/grade leaves by size, colour, and texture.
Bale into 50-60 kg bales; press using tobacco press.
Store in humid warehouse (65% RH) to prevent brittleness.
Fermentation/aging for 1-2 years for flue-cured tobacco.
Market through Tobacco Board auctions.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Potato
steps: Well-drained sandy loam to loam; pH 5.5–6.5.
Deep plough to 25-30 cm; cross harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 20-25 tonnes/ha FYM; incorporate.
Form ridges 50-60 cm apart (for planting tubers on ridges).

step_2_seed_tuber_selection.title: Seed Tuber Selection & Treatment
steps: Use certified disease-free seed tubers (30-50 g weight).
Varieties: Kufri Jyoti, Kufri Pukhraj, Kufri Chandramukhi.
Seed rate: 2-2.5 tonnes/ha.
Pre-sprouting: Store at 15-20°C for 20-25 days in diffused light.
Treat with Mancozeb 0.2% + Streptocycline 100 ppm for 15 minutes.
Cut large tubers into 40-50 g pieces with 2-3 eyes.

step_3_planting.title: Planting
steps: Plant on ridges at 50-60 cm row spacing.
Plant spacing: 20-25 cm within row.
Depth: 5-7 cm; cover with 3-4 cm soil.
Basal: N:P:K = 120:80:100 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation Schedule
steps: Irrigate immediately after planting for sprouting.
Critical: At tuber initiation (Day 40-50) and tuber bulking (Day 60-80).
Irrigate every 5-7 days in winter; 7-10 days in spring.
Stop irrigation 10-15 days before harvest.
Avoid waterlogging — causes tuber rot.

schedule.fertilizers: DAP 174 kg/ha + MOP 167 kg/ha + Urea 130 kg/ha (N:P:K = 120:80:100)
Earth up soil around plants to cover tubers; prevents greening

diseases.control: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre; spray at 5-7 day intervals in humid weather
Use disease-free seed; crop rotation with non-solanaceous crops

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when leaves turn yellow and dry.
Stop irrigation 10-15 days before harvest.
Cut haulms (above ground parts) 10 days before harvest to toughen skin.
Dig with spade or potato digger.
Sort tubers; grade by size.
Expected yield: 25-35 tonnes/ha.

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Dry tubers in shade for 4-5 hours (not sun — greening).
Cure at 15-20°C, 85-90% RH for 10-15 days to heal wounds.
Store in cool, dark, ventilated store at 2-4°C, 85-90% RH.
Grade: Size A > 55 mm; Size B 35-55 mm; Size C < 35 mm.
Market fresh or process into chips/fries.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Onion (Rabi Type)
steps: Well-drained sandy loam to loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
Deep plough once; cross harrow 2-3 times.
Apply 15-20 tonnes/ha FYM; incorporate.
Form raised beds (1 m wide × 10 m long) for irrigation.

step_2_nursery_and_seed_treatment.title: Nursery Preparation
steps: Seed rate: 8-10 kg/ha.
Treat seeds with Carbendazim 2g/kg + Thiram 2g/kg.
Soak seeds in water for 12 hours before sowing for uniform germination.
Sow on raised nursery beds at spacing 5-6 cm × 2 cm.
Cover with fine soil + FYM mixture.

step_3_transplanting.title: Transplanting
steps: Transplant seedlings at 15 cm × 10 cm spacing.
Irrigate immediately after transplanting.
Basal: N:P:K = 50:60:50 kg/ha.

fertilizer_schedule.fertilizers: SSP 375 kg + MOP 83 kg + Urea 109 kg per ha

pests.control: Spinosad 45 SC @ 73 ml/ha OR Fipronil 5 SC @ 600 ml/ha

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when 50-60% tops fall over (neck softening).
Pull bulbs manually or with mechanical harvester.
Dry in field for 2-3 days (curing).
Trim roots and leaves leaving 2-3 cm neck.
Expected yield: 25-40 tonnes/ha.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Complete curing: 15-20 days in shade with good ventilation.
Store in well-ventilated, dry store at 25-30°C, 65-70% RH.
Grade by bulb size: Extra Large > 80 mm; Large 60-80 mm; Medium 40-60 mm.
Shelf life: 3-4 months for Rabi onion.
Market fresh or process into dehydrated onion flakes/powder.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Garlic
steps: Sandy loam to loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
Deep plough once; 2-3 harrowings.
Apply 15 tonnes/ha FYM; incorporate.

step_2_clove_selection_and_treatment.title: Clove Selection & Treatment
steps: Select large, healthy cloves from certified seed.
Varieties: Yamuna Safed-3, G-50, Agrifound White.
Seed rate: 400-500 kg/ha.
Soak cloves in Carbendazim 0.1% for 12 hours before planting.
Soak in water for 8 hours before planting for better germination.

step_3_planting.title: Planting
steps: Row spacing: 15-20 cm; plant spacing: 7-10 cm.
Plant cloves at 3-4 cm depth with pointed side up.
Basal: N:P:K = 60:50:100 kg/ha.

fertilizer_schedule.fertilizers: SSP 312 kg + MOP 167 kg + Urea 130 kg per ha

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when 50-60% leaves turn yellow and dry.
Pull bulbs manually.
Dry in field for 5-7 days.
Trim roots and leaves.
Expected yield: 8-12 tonnes/ha.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Complete curing: 15-20 days in shade.
Store in ventilated bags at 20-25°C, 60-65% RH.
Shelf life: 5-6 months.
Grade by clove size for seed purpose.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Carrot
steps: Deep, well-drained sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
Deep plough to 30-40 cm (remove stones for straight roots).
Apply 20-25 tonnes/ha FYM.
Form raised beds.

step_2_seed_treatment_and_sowing.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
steps: Varieties: Pusa Kesar, Nantes, Pusa Rudhira.
Seed rate: 8-10 kg/ha.
Treat with Thiram 3g/kg seed.
Row spacing: 30 cm; plant spacing: 7-10 cm.
Sowing depth: 1-2 cm (seeds small).
Mix seeds with sand 1:4 for uniform sowing.

step_3_irrigation_and_fertilizer.title: Irrigation & Fertilizer
irrigation_steps: Irrigate every 5-7 days during root development (Day 50-90).
Avoid water stress at root initiation.

fertilizer_schedule.fertilizers: KNO3 1% foliar at root bulking for sweetness

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when roots reach 2.5-3 cm diameter.
Irrigate 2 days before for easy pulling.
Pull manually; trim leaves to 2 cm.
Expected yield: 25-40 tonnes/ha.

step_6_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Wash; remove soil.
Store at 0-4°C, 90-95% RH for 4-6 months.
Grade by length and diameter.
Process: juice, dehydrated, frozen.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation for Radish
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
Deep plough to 25-30 cm.
Apply 15 tonnes/ha FYM.

step_2_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Varieties: Pusa Chetki, Pusa Himani, Japanese White.
Seed rate: 8-10 kg/ha.
Row spacing: 30-45 cm; plant spacing: 10 cm.
Sowing depth: 2-3 cm.

step_3_fertilizer_and_irrigation.title: Fertilizer & Irrigation
irrigation_steps: Irrigate every 4-5 days for tender roots.

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when root diameter 2.5-3 cm.
Pull manually; remove leaves.
Expected yield: 15-20 tonnes/ha.

step_1_soil_preparation_and_nursery.title: Soil Prep & Nursery
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–6.5.
Seed rate: 500-600 g/ha.
Nursery: Sow in raised beds; transplant at 35-40 days.

step_2_transplanting_and_fertilizer.title: Transplanting & Fertilizer
steps: Spacing: 45 cm × 45 cm.
Basal: N:P:K = 100:60:60 kg/ha.
Top dressing: Urea 65 kg/ha at Day 30 and 55.

pests.control: Spinosad 45 SC @ 73 ml/ha OR Btk 1 kg/ha

diseases.control: Lime application (raise pH to 7.0); avoid cabbage family rotation

step_4_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when heads are firm and solid.
Cut head with 2-3 wrapper leaves.
Expected yield: 30-40 tonnes/ha.

step_1_nursery_and_transplanting.title: Nursery & Transplanting
steps: Seed rate: 500-600 g/ha.
Sow in nursery; transplant at 35-40 days.
Spacing: 45 cm × 45 cm.

step_4_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when curd is compact, white, and 10-15 cm diameter.
Cut curd with 2-3 wrapper leaves.
Expected yield: 20-30 tonnes/ha.

step_1_soil_preparation_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.5–7.5.
Seed rate: 40-50 kg/ha.
Broadcast or row sowing at 25 cm spacing.
Treat seed with Thiram 3g/kg.

step_4_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when leaves are 10-15 cm.
Cut leaves 2-3 cm above ground.
Expected yield: 12-15 tonnes/ha.

step_1_soil_preparation_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
Seed rate: 25-30 kg/ha.
Broadcast or row sowing at 20-25 cm spacing.
Soak seeds in water for 8-12 hours before sowing.

step_4_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest for greens at 30-35 days.
For seed: harvest at 60 days when pods turn brown.
Expected yield: 8-10 tonnes/ha (greens).

step_1_soil_preparation_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
Deep plough to 30 cm.
Seed rate: 8-10 kg/ha.
Row spacing: 30-45 cm; plant spacing: 10 cm.
Soak seeds in water for 12 hours before sowing.

step_4_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest when root diameter 5-7 cm.
Pull manually; trim leaves.
Expected yield: 25-35 tonnes/ha.

step_1_soil_preparation_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Clay loam; pH 6.5–8.0.
Plough once; harrow 2 times.
Apply 5 tonnes/ha FYM.
Seed rate: 25 kg/ha (un-inoculated); 20 kg/ha (inoculated).
Inoculate with Rhizobium trifolii.
Row spacing: 20-25 cm; broadcast also works.

fertilizer_schedule.fertilizers: P2O5 60 kg/ha + K2O 40 kg/ha + N 20 kg/ha

step_3_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: First cut at 50-60 days after sowing (30-40 cm height).
Subsequent cuts every 25-30 days.
Cut 5-7 cm above ground.
Expected yield: 80-120 tonnes/ha green fodder.

step_1_soil_preparation_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Deep, well-drained loamy soil; pH 6.5–7.5.
Deep plough to 30 cm.
Apply 10 tonnes/ha FYM.
Seed rate: 20-25 kg/ha (inoculated).
Inoculate with Rhizobium meliloti.
Row spacing: 25-30 cm; depth 2-3 cm.

fertilizer_schedule.fertilizers: P2O5 80 kg/ha + K2O 60 kg/ha + N 30 kg/ha

step_3_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: First cut at 60-70 days (50-60 cm height).
Subsequent cuts every 28-35 days.
Cut 5-7 cm above ground.
Expected yield: 100-150 tonnes/ha green fodder/year.

step_1_sowing_and_fertilizer.title: Sowing & Fertilizer
steps: Seed rate: 100-120 kg/ha.
Row spacing: 20-25 cm.
Basal: N:P:K = 60:40:40 kg/ha.

step_2_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest at 50% flowering stage for best quality.
Cut at ground level.
Expected yield: 35-45 tonnes/ha green fodder.
